title: Natural Gas Demand Spike From Unseasonable Cold Snap Detection
thesis: Daily minimum temperatures drop >15% below 30-day rolling average in winter months, signaling heating demand surge and natural gas consumption pressure. Utilities and energy infrastructure see immediate margin expansion from peak demand pricing and capacity utilization.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly for US population centers through weather_series adapter
signal_logic: When US population-weighted temperature anomaly drops below -2 standard deviations from seasonal norm for 2+ consecutive days
entry_exit: Entry: When US population-weighted temperature anomaly drops below -2 standard deviations from seasonal norm for 2+ consecutive days Exit: When temperature anomaly recovers above -1 standard deviation or after 7 trading days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly for US population centers through weather_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter weather volatility is seasonal and repeats reliably; unseasonable cold snaps occur multiple times per winter season.

schema.idea: Natural gas demand spike from unseasonable cold snap detection

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly for US population centers through weather_series adapter

schema.behavior: Daily minimum temperatures drop >15% below 30-day rolling average in winter months, signaling heating demand surge and natural gas consumption pressure.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities and energy infrastructure see immediate margin expansion from peak demand pricing and capacity utilization.

trade_logic.entry: When US population-weighted temperature anomaly drops below -2 standard deviations from seasonal norm for 2+ consecutive days
trade_logic.exit: When temperature anomaly recovers above -1 standard deviation or after 7 trading days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter weather volatility is seasonal and repeats reliably; unseasonable cold snaps occur multiple times per winter season.

title: Google Trends Surge In Mortgage Refinance Calculator Signals Rate-sensitive Financial Stress
thesis: Household financial decision-making spikes when mortgage rates move sharply; refinance inquiry volume predicts consumer confidence and housing demand shifts 1-2 weeks ahead. Rising refinance inquiry volume signals rate shock and housing affordability stress, which historically precedes financial sector weakness.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'mortgage refinance calculator' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: When weekly search volume exceeds 80th percentile of 52-week rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: When weekly search volume exceeds 80th percentile of 52-week rolling average Exit: When search volume falls below 60th percentile over 2 consecutive weeks or after 14 days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'mortgage refinance calculator' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rate volatility is endemic to markets; mortgage rate moves trigger search spikes reliably every 3-4 weeks on average.

schema.idea: Google Trends surge in 'mortgage refinance calculator' signals rate-sensitive financial stress

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'mortgage refinance calculator' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Household financial decision-making spikes when mortgage rates move sharply; refinance inquiry volume predicts consumer confidence and housing demand shifts 1-2 weeks ahead.

market_impact.rationale: Rising refinance inquiry volume signals rate shock and housing affordability stress, which historically precedes financial sector weakness.

trade_logic.entry: When weekly search volume exceeds 80th percentile of 52-week rolling average
trade_logic.exit: When search volume falls below 60th percentile over 2 consecutive weeks or after 14 days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rate volatility is endemic to markets; mortgage rate moves trigger search spikes reliably every 3-4 weeks on average.

title: Weekly Ev Charger Network Density Jump In Rural Counties
thesis: Rural county EV charger deployments cluster in specific weeks as infrastructure grants roll out; rapid county-level density changes signal local economic stimulus and tech adoption momentum. Infrastructure build-out and manufacturing for EV charging hardware drives industrial production and materials demand.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap charger counts by county through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: When a county's week-over-week charger count increases by >5 net new units and density is >20% above county 52-week average
entry_exit: Entry: When a county's week-over-week charger count increases by >5 net new units and density is >20% above county 52-week average Exit: After 10 trading days or when density falls below 15% above average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap charger counts by county through openchargemap adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Federal and state EV infrastructure programs release deployment tranches in predictable waves; clusters occur 2-3 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Weekly EV charger network density jump in rural counties

data.source: OpenChargeMap charger counts by county through openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: Rural county EV charger deployments cluster in specific weeks as infrastructure grants roll out; rapid county-level density changes signal local economic stimulus and tech adoption momentum.

market_impact.rationale: Infrastructure build-out and manufacturing for EV charging hardware drives industrial production and materials demand.

trade_logic.entry: When a county's week-over-week charger count increases by >5 net new units and density is >20% above county 52-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when density falls below 15% above average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Federal and state EV infrastructure programs release deployment tranches in predictable waves; clusters occur 2-3 times per quarter.

title: Freight Rail Car Utilization Bounce After Seasonal Trough
thesis: Post-holiday seasonal freight collapse (Q1) reverses sharply as spring manufacturing ramps; rail car utilization jumps >15% week-over-week signal economic recovery confidence. Rail utilization surge indicates industrial production recovery and demand acceleration across consumer goods and materials.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car orders and loads through html_table adapter
signal_logic: When weekly freight car loads exceed 52-week average by >8% after 4+ weeks below average
entry_exit: Entry: When weekly freight car loads exceed 52-week average by >8% after 4+ weeks below average Exit: After 3 weeks of sustained above-average loads or after 21 days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly freight car orders and loads through html_table adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal freight cycles repeat annually; Q1-to-Q2 transition produces reliable utilization spikes multiple times per year in the dataset.

schema.idea: Freight rail car utilization bounce after seasonal trough

data.source: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car orders and loads through html_table adapter

schema.behavior: Post-holiday seasonal freight collapse (Q1) reverses sharply as spring manufacturing ramps; rail car utilization jumps >15% week-over-week signal economic recovery confidence.

market_impact.rationale: Rail utilization surge indicates industrial production recovery and demand acceleration across consumer goods and materials.

trade_logic.entry: When weekly freight car loads exceed 52-week average by >8% after 4+ weeks below average
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks of sustained above-average loads or after 21 days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal freight cycles repeat annually; Q1-to-Q2 transition produces reliable utilization spikes multiple times per year in the dataset.

title: Rss News Volume Spike On Corporate Bond Downgrades Signals Credit Stress Rotation
thesis: Daily spike in downgrade-related news articles (>150% of 20-day average) predicts 3-5 day financials sector weakness as credit risk reprices. Downgrade news cascades trigger flight-to-quality and de-risking in credit-sensitive financials.
data_sources: RSS feed counts mentioning 'credit downgrade' OR 'bond rating cut' through rss_count adapter
signal_logic: When daily RSS count exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average for 'credit downgrade' keywords
entry_exit: Entry: When daily RSS count exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average for 'credit downgrade' keywords Exit: After 5 trading days or when RSS count normalizes below 110% of average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed counts mentioning 'credit downgrade' OR 'bond rating cut' through rss_count adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit events and rating changes occur multiple times per month across corporate universe; news spikes reliably follow.

schema.idea: RSS news volume spike on corporate bond downgrades signals credit stress rotation

data.source: RSS feed counts mentioning 'credit downgrade' OR 'bond rating cut' through rss_count adapter

market_impact.rationale: Downgrade news cascades trigger flight-to-quality and de-risking in credit-sensitive financials.

trade_logic.entry: When daily RSS count exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average for 'credit downgrade' keywords
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or when RSS count normalizes below 110% of average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit events and rating changes occur multiple times per month across corporate universe; news spikes reliably follow.

title: Google Trends Surge In Logistics Job Openings Signals Labor Shortage In Freight
thesis: Weekly spikes in job search interest for logistics roles correlate with labor shortages and wage inflation pressure; signals upcoming freight cost increases. Labor shortage signals compress margins for transportation and warehousing-dependent industrials.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'warehouse job' OR 'truck driver jobs' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: When weekly search volume exceeds 75th percentile of 52-week range for logistics job keywords
entry_exit: Entry: When weekly search volume exceeds 75th percentile of 52-week range for logistics job keywords Exit: When search volume falls below 60th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks or after 14 days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'warehouse job' OR 'truck driver jobs' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal labor demand in logistics peaks predictably; search spikes occur weekly during peak hiring seasons (Q4, spring).

title: Vix-to-realized Volatility Contraction Signals Mean Reversion Opportunity In Large Caps
thesis: When VIX futures curve inverts (short-term > long-term) while 20-day realized vol on SPY is <50% of VIX level, market is overpricing tail risk; mean reversion long bias. Volatility contraction and fear premium collapse drive equity risk appetite rotation into growth and technology.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for SPY and VIX close through price_only adapter
signal_logic: When VIX closes >15% above 20-day realized volatility of SPY and VIX is in top quartile of 252-day range
entry_exit: Entry: When VIX closes >15% above 20-day realized volatility of SPY and VIX is in top quartile of 252-day range Exit: When VIX-to-realized ratio normalizes to median or after 7 trading days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for SPY and VIX close through price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility term structure inversions and fear premium spikes occur 2-3 times per month in normal market regimes.

schema.idea: VIX-to-realized volatility contraction signals mean reversion opportunity in large caps

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for SPY and VIX close through price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When VIX futures curve inverts (short-term > long-term) while 20-day realized vol on SPY is <50% of VIX level, market is overpricing tail risk; mean reversion long bias.

market_impact.rationale: Volatility contraction and fear premium collapse drive equity risk appetite rotation into growth and technology.

trade_logic.entry: When VIX closes >15% above 20-day realized volatility of SPY and VIX is in top quartile of 252-day range
trade_logic.exit: When VIX-to-realized ratio normalizes to median or after 7 trading days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility term structure inversions and fear premium spikes occur 2-3 times per month in normal market regimes.

title: Fred Unemployment Insurance Claims Weekly Print Beats Expectations Triggers Defensive Rotation
thesis: When weekly initial jobless claims exceed consensus forecast by >5% (using 4-week smoothed trend), markets reprices labor risk; defensive sectors outperform 3-5 days. Claims beats signal economic softening and consumer income pressure; discretionary spending faces headwinds.
data_sources: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: When weekly ICSA print >5% above 8-week moving average and >110% of prior year same week
entry_exit: Entry: When weekly ICSA print >5% above 8-week moving average and >110% of prior year same week Exit: When ICSA normalizes back within 2% of 8-week average or after 10 trading days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market volatility produces claims surprises multiple times per quarter; seasonal patterns are well-established.

schema.idea: FRED unemployment insurance claims weekly print beats expectations triggers defensive rotation

data.source: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When weekly initial jobless claims exceed consensus forecast by >5% (using 4-week smoothed trend), markets reprices labor risk; defensive sectors outperform 3-5 days.

market_impact.rationale: Claims beats signal economic softening and consumer income pressure; discretionary spending faces headwinds.

trade_logic.entry: When weekly ICSA print >5% above 8-week moving average and >110% of prior year same week
trade_logic.exit: When ICSA normalizes back within 2% of 8-week average or after 10 trading days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market volatility produces claims surprises multiple times per quarter; seasonal patterns are well-established.

title: Earthquake Activity Surge In Oil-producing Regions Signals Supply Disruption Risk Premium
thesis: When seismic activity (M>3.5) near shale basins increases >200% vs. 30-day average, energy markets price in production disruption risk; crude rallies 2-3%. Earthquake risk near drilling operations creates supply uncertainty and commodity risk premium expansion.
data_sources: USGS earthquake magnitude and location data for Texas, Oklahoma, New Mexico through earthquake_activity adapter
signal_logic: When daily earthquake count in oil-producing regions exceeds 200% of 30-day rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: When daily earthquake count in oil-producing regions exceeds 200% of 30-day rolling average Exit: After 5 trading days or when earthquake count normalizes below 130% of average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake magnitude and location data for Texas, Oklahoma, New Mexico through earthquake_activity adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clusters in shale regions occur sporadically but reliably several times per year; each triggers 2-5 day crude rallies.

schema.idea: Earthquake activity surge in oil-producing regions signals supply disruption risk premium

data.source: USGS earthquake magnitude and location data for Texas, Oklahoma, New Mexico through earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: When seismic activity (M>3.5) near shale basins increases >200% vs. 30-day average, energy markets price in production disruption risk; crude rallies 2-3%.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake risk near drilling operations creates supply uncertainty and commodity risk premium expansion.

trade_logic.entry: When daily earthquake count in oil-producing regions exceeds 200% of 30-day rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or when earthquake count normalizes below 130% of average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clusters in shale regions occur sporadically but reliably several times per year; each triggers 2-5 day crude rallies.
